blocks:
Ayah

سَبَّحَ لِلَّهِ مَا فِى ٱلسَّمَـٰوَٰتِ وَمَا فِى ٱلْأَرْضِ ۖ وَهُوَ ٱلْعَزِيزُ ٱلْحَكِيمُ (۱)
Whatever there is in the heavens and the earth glorifies Allah, and He is the All-mighty, the All-wise. (1)

Messages

1- In the Islamic perspective, all existence possesses consciousness, and all are engaged in glorifying Allah. «سبّح للّه ما...»

2- The One worthy of glorification and sanctification is He who possesses infinite power and unparalleled wisdom. «و هو العزیز الحكیم»

Explanations

| Portrait of Surah As-Saff This Surah has fourteen verses (Ayahs) and is one of the Medinan Surahs. Due to the word «صفّ» (Saff), which appears in the fourth verse, this Surah has been named "Saff." The main focus of this Surah is on the superiority of the religion of Islam over other heavenly religions, the necessity of striving (Jihad) in the way of Allah, and supporting the Divine Prophets. In addition to this Surah, Surahs Al-Hadid and Al-Hashr also begin with the word «سَبَّح», and Surahs Al-Jumu'ah and At-Taghabun with the word «یُسبِّح», and Surah Al-Isra with the word «سبحان», and Surah Al-A'la begins with the command «سَبِّح اسم ربّك».

Ayah

يَـٰٓأَيُّهَا ٱلَّذِينَ ءَامَنُوا۟ لِمَ تَقُولُونَ مَا لَا تَفْعَلُونَ (۲)
O you who have faith! Why do you say what you do not do? (2)

Ayah

كَبُرَ مَقْتًا عِندَ ٱللَّهِ أَن تَقُولُوا۟ مَا لَا تَفْعَلُونَ (۳)
It is greatly outrageous to Allah that you should say what you do not do. (3)

Key Points

Maqt (مَقت) means intense anger and wrath. A similar reproach is found in the Ayah: «أتأمرون النّاس بالبرّ و تنسون أنفسكم» (1). Failure to act upon one's words is sometimes due to inability, and sometimes due to indifference, the latter being subject to reproach.

The scholar without action is likened to a fruitless tree, a rainless cloud, a waterless river, a bee without honey, a needle without thread, and a donkey carrying books. In the narrations (Hadiths), we read: «علم بلاعمل حجة اللّه على العبد» (2). Knowledge without action is a proof (argument) against the servant. «العلم الّذى لایُعمل به كالكنز الّذى لاینفق منه» (3). Knowledge that is not acted upon is like a treasure from which nothing is spent or utilized. «انّ اشد الناس حسرة یوم القیامة الّذى وصفوا العدل ثمّ خالفوه» (4). The person who speaks of justice but is not just will have the most intense regret on the Day of Resurrection. Prophet Jesus عليه السلام said: «أشقى الناس مَن هو معروف عند النّاس بعلمه مجهول بعمله» (5). The person who is known among people for his knowledge but unknown for his actions is the most wretched of people. Imam Sadiq عليه السلام said: «من لم یُصدّق فعله قوله فلیس بعالم» (6). The person whose actions do not confirm his words is not a scholar.

Imam Sadiq عليه السلام considered breaking a promise (خلف وعده) to be one of the manifestations of this Ayah, and He said: The promise of a believer to his brother is like a vow, but (violating it) has no expiation (kaffarah). «عدة المؤمن اخاه نذرٌ لا كفّارة له» (7). According to the commentators, the occasion of revelation (Sha'n Nuzul) for the Ayah concerns those who desired Jihad, but when the command for Jihad was issued, they made excuses and apologies. However, the content of the Ayah relates to all those who give slogans but do not act.

1) Baqarah, 44. 2) Ghurar al-Hikam. 3) Amali Saduq, p. 343. 4) Bihar, Vol. 2, p. 30. 5) Misbah ash-Shari'ah, p. 368. 6) Kafi, Vol. 1, p. 36. 7) Kafi, Vol. 2, p. 363.

Messages

1- Faith must be accompanied by action and sincerity, otherwise, one deserves blame and reproach. (The entire Ayah)

2- Before criticizing, address the positive points. You who believe, why do you not act? «یا ایها الّذین آمنوا... لِمَ... لا تفعلون»

3- One of the methods of education (tarbiyah) is timely and appropriate reproach. «لم تقولون ما لا تفعلون»

4- Fulfilling a promise is obligatory (wajib), and breaking a promise (khulf-e wa'dah) is among the major sins (kabirah). «كبر مقتاً»

5- How ugly it is that the entire existence is glorifying Allah, «سبّح للّه», yet man provokes Allah's wrath through words without action. «كبر مقتاً عند اللّه»

6- Speech without action is dangerous. «كبر مقتاً عند اللّه ان تقولوا ما لاتفعلون» (The words kabura, maqtan, and ‘inda Allah indicate the seriousness of the matter.)

7- Even if your affairs are resolved today through false promises and slogans, you will face a dangerous situation before Allah. «كبر مقتاً عند اللّه...»

Ayah

إِنَّ ٱللَّهَ يُحِبُّ ٱلَّذِينَ يُقَـٰتِلُونَ فِى سَبِيلِهِۦ صَفًّا كَأَنَّهُم بُنْيَـٰنٌ مَّرْصُوصٌ (٤)
Indeed Allah loves those who fight in His way in ranks, as if they were a compact structure. (4)

Key Points

Bunyaan (بنیان) means structure or building. Marṣūṣ (مرصوص) comes from raṣāṣ (رصاص), meaning lead. Bunyaan Marṣūṣ (بنیان مرصوص) means a structure that, due to its extreme solidity, appears to be made of lead. Perhaps the previous Ayah was a criticism of those who raised the slogan of Jihad and fighting but failed to act upon those slogans. Therefore, Allah said that those who did not act are subject to wrath, «كَبُرَ مقتاً» (it is greatly hateful). However, in this Ayah, He praises those who fulfilled their slogans and promises and considers them beloved by Allah. «ان اللّه یحبّ الّذین یقاتلون» It is recorded in history that during the Battle of Siffin, Hadrat Amīr عليه السلام recited this Ayah to boost the morale of His companions.(8)

8) Tafsir Rūḥ al-Ma‘ānī.

Messages

1- In education and training, both wrath (deterrence) and mercy (encouragement) are necessary. (In the previous Ayah, the discussion was about Divine wrath, and here it is about Divine mercy). «انّ اللّه یحبّ...»

2- Warning (admonition) is necessary to deter people from undesirable actions, «لِمَ تقولون... كَبُر مقتاً» (Why do you say... it is greatly hateful), but encouragement is necessary to motivate them toward good deeds. «یحبّ الّذین یقاتلون» (He loves those who fight).

3- Attaining the pleasure and love of Allah is the highest encouragement for believers, even superior to Paradise and similar rewards. «انّ اللّه یحبّ...»

4- Difficult tasks require strong motivation, and the highest motivation is becoming beloved by Allah. «انّ اللّه یحبّ الّذین یقاتلون»

5- Believing Mujahidin (strugglers) are the lovers of Allah and fight in His way, «یقاتلون فى سبیله» (they fight in His way), and Allah also loves them. «یحبّ الّذین یقاتلون»

6- Under normal circumstances, unity, alliance, and avoidance of division are necessary, but when facing the enemy, the ranks of the believers must be even more united, impenetrable, and solid. «صفّاً كانّهم بنیان مرصوص»

Ayah

وَإِذْ قَالَ مُوسَىٰ لِقَوْمِهِۦ يَـٰقَوْمِ لِمَ تُؤْذُونَنِى وَقَد تَّعْلَمُونَ أَنِّى رَسُولُ ٱللَّهِ إِلَيْكُمْ ۖ فَلَمَّا زَاغُوٓا۟ أَزَاغَ ٱللَّهُ قُلُوبَهُمْ ۚ وَٱللَّهُ لَا يَهْدِى ٱلْقَوْمَ ٱلْفَـٰسِقِينَ (٥)
When Moses said to his people, ‘O my people! Why do you torment me, when you certainly know that I am Allah’s apostle to you?’ So when they swerved [from the right path], Allah made their hearts swerve, and Allah does not guide the transgressing lot. (5)

Key Points

Zāgha (زاغَ) derives from Zaygh (زیغ), meaning deviation from the path of truth. The human soul is inclined toward the straight path, and it is man who deviates it. In the previous verses, the criticism was directed at speech without action; in this verse, the criticism is directed at action contrary to knowledge—why do they torment and harm him despite knowing that he is the Prophet of Allah?

Allah establishes reward and punishment based on His justice and wisdom and the performance of man. He guides people through intellect (‘aql), innate nature (fitrah), and the sending of Prophets. Whoever accepts guidance, He increases him in guidance: «والّذین اهتدوا زادهم هدى » (9). And whoever knowingly and willfully chooses the path of deviation, Allah abandons him. «زاغوا ازاغ اللّه»

The Torment and Harm Inflicted upon Prophet Moses and the Holy Prophet (Muhammad)

The torments inflicted upon Moses عليه السلام by his people were numerous. Among them, they said: «لن نؤمن لك حتّى نرى اللّه جهرة» (10) (We will never believe you until we see Allah openly.) «لن نصبر على طعام واحد» (11) (We will not endure a single kind of food.) «لن ندخلها حتّى یخرجوا منها» (12) (We will not enter it until they leave it.) «فاذهب انت و ربّك فقاتلا انّا هاهنا قاعدون» (13) (Go forth, you and your Lord, and fight; we are sitting right here.) «اجعل لنا الهاً كما لهم آلهة» (14) (Appoint for us a god just as they have gods.) «قالوا اوذینا من قبل ان تأتینا و من بعد ما جئتنا» (15) (O Moses! We were tormented both before you came to us and after you came to us.)

The Holy Prophet صلى الله علیه وآله was also subjected to torment and harm by the polytheists and hypocrites, as the Quran states: «و منهم الّذین یؤذون النّبى و یقولون هو اُذن» (16) (And among them [the hypocrites] are those who constantly harm the Prophet and say, "He is all ear" [i.e., gullible].) «والّذین یؤذون رسول اللّه لهم عذابٌ الیم» (17) (For those who harm the Messenger of Allah, a painful punishment is prepared and ready.) «ان ذلكم كان یؤذى النّبىّ فیستحیى منكم» (18) (Indeed, that action of yours harms the Prophet, and he is shy of you [to express his discomfort].)

Another form of harm that the Prophet of Islam endured was the sloth of some Muslims regarding Jihad. They would say: «انّ بیوتنا عورة» (19) (Our houses are exposed/unprotected), «لاتنفروا فى الحّر» (20) (Do not go forth in the heat), «بَعُدت علیهم الشقة» (21) (The distance is far), «و لاتفتنّى الا فى الفتنة سقطوا» (22) (By participating in the Battle of Tabuk, we might look upon Roman women and girls and fall into temptation), and [they complained that] weapons and resources were scarce, etc.

Considering the previous verses, which stated why you do not act upon your words and that Allah loves those who fight, it becomes clear that the intended meaning of "harm" in this verse is sloth in Jihad and reluctance to go to the front lines.

9) Muhammad, 17. 10) Al-Baqarah, 55. 11) Al-Baqarah, 61. 12) Al-Ma’idah, 22. 13) Al-Ma’idah, 24. 14) Al-A‘raf, 138. 15) Al-A‘raf, 129. 16) At-Tawbah, 61. 17) At-Tawbah, 61. 18) Al-Ahzab, 53. 19) Al-Ahzab, 13. 20) At-Tawbah, 81. 21) At-Tawbah, 42. 22) At-Tawbah, 49.

Messages

1- The history of the life of Prophet Moses عليه السلام should not be forgotten due to the lessons and warnings it contains. «و اذ...»

2- Familiarity with the history of great figures provides comfort and solace. «اذ قال موسى لقومه... لِمَ تؤذوننى»

3- Sometimes insiders create more problems than outsiders. «یا قوم لِمَ تؤذوننى»

4- In forbidding evil (Nahy ‘anil Munkar), use the leverage of emotion/sympathy. «یا قوم لِمَ تؤذوننى»

5- The harm inflicted by followers should not lead to abandoning or rejecting them. «یا قوم لِمَ تؤذوننى»

6- Knowledge and awareness bring responsibility. The knowledgeable are expected to act upon their knowledge. «لِمَ تؤذوننى و قد تعلمون...»

7- Speech and behavior that cause harm to religious leaders lead to human deviation. «لِمَ تؤذوننى... زاغوا»

8- Deviation in speech and behavior is the prelude to the deviation of man's heart, soul, personality, identity, culture, and thought. «فلمّا زاغوا ازاغ اللّه قلوبهم»

9- Sin results in deprivation from Divine guidance. «واللّه لایهدى القوم الفاسقین»

Ayah

وَإِذْ قَالَ عِيسَى ٱبْنُ مَرْيَمَ يَـٰبَنِىٓ إِسْرَٰٓءِيلَ إِنِّى رَسُولُ ٱللَّهِ إِلَيْكُم مُّصَدِّقًا لِّمَا بَيْنَ يَدَىَّ مِنَ ٱلتَّوْرَىٰةِ وَمُبَشِّرًۢا بِرَسُولٍ يَأْتِى مِنۢ بَعْدِى ٱسْمُهُۥٓ أَحْمَدُ ۖ فَلَمَّا جَآءَهُم بِٱلْبَيِّنَـٰتِ قَالُوا۟ هَـٰذَا سِحْرٌ مُّبِينٌ (٦)
And when Jesus son of Mary said, ‘O Children of Israel! Indeed I am the apostle of Allah to you, to confirm what is before me of the Torah, and to give the good news of an apostle who will come after me, whose name is Ahmad.’ But when he brought them manifest proofs, they said, ‘This is plain magic.’ (6)

Key Points

Undoubtedly, what is currently in the hands of Jews and Christians under the name of the Torah and the Gospel are not the books revealed to the great Prophets of God, Moses and Jesus (عليهما السلام), but rather a collection of books compiled by their companions or individuals born after them. A brief study of these books is living proof of this claim, and the Jews and Christians themselves do not claim more than this. Of course, there is no doubt that part of the teachings of Moses and Jesus and the content of their books have been transferred to these books through the sayings of their followers. For this reason, one cannot deny everything that has come in the Old Testament (the Torah and related books) and the New Testament (the Gospel and related books); rather, the content of the aforementioned books is a mixture of the teachings of these two great Prophets of God and the thoughts and ideas of others. Various expressions are seen in the existing books that herald the advent of a great Prophet in the future, whose signs apply only to the Prophet of Islam. It is interesting that, apart from the prophecies found in these books that apply to the person of the Prophet of Islam صلى الله عليه وآله, the word "Paraclete" (فارقلیط) is emphasized in three instances in the Gospel of John, which has been translated into Persian as "Comforter" (تسلّى دهنده). Now pay attention to the text of the Gospel of John, quoted from Tafsir Namuneh: "And I will ask the Father, and He will give you another 'Comforter' who will abide with you forever."(23) And in Chapter 15 it states: "But when the 'Comforter' comes... He will testify about Me."(24) And in Chapter 16 we also read: "Nevertheless, I tell you the truth; it is expedient for you that I go away: for if I go not away, the 'Comforter' will not come unto you; but if I depart, I will send Him unto you."(25) The important point is that in the Syriac text of the Gospels, which is derived from the Greek original, the word "Parqleeta" (پارقلیطا) appears instead of "Comforter," and in the Greek text, it is "Periclytos" (پیركلتوس), which, according to Greek culture, means "the praised person" and is equivalent to the words "Muhammad" and "Ahmad" in the Arabic language. But when the church authorities realized that the dissemination of such a translation would severely damage their establishment, they wrote the word "Paracletos" (پاراكلتوس), meaning "Comforter," instead of "Periclytos." With this clear distortion, they altered this living document, although, despite this distortion, a clear prophecy of the advent of a great Prophet in the future is still evident.(26) In the Great French Encyclopedia, it is stated regarding this matter: "Muhammad is the founder of the religion of Islam, the Messenger of God, and the Seal of the Prophets. The word 'Muhammad' means 'highly praised' and is derived from the root 'Hamd,' meaning glorification and praise. Another name, also derived from the root 'Hamd' and synonymous with the word 'Muhammad,' is 'Ahmad.' It is highly probable that the Christians of Arabia used this word instead of 'Paraclete.' Ahmad means 'highly praised' and 'highly glorified,' which is the translation of the word 'Periclytos,' and the word 'Paracletos' was mistakenly substituted for it. Thus, Muslim religious writers have repeatedly pointed out that the intent of this word is the prophecy of the advent of the Prophet of Islam. The Holy Quran also clearly refers to this matter in the sixth verse of Surah As-Saff."(27)

Was the name of the Prophet of Islam, "Ahmad"?

A) It is stated in historical books that the Prophet of Islam صلى الله عليه وآله had two names since childhood, and people addressed him by both: one was "Muhammad" and the other was "Ahmad." The first name was chosen by the Prophet's grandfather, Abdul Muttalib, and the name Ahmad was chosen by his mother, Aminah. This matter is detailed in Sirah Halabi.

B) One of those who repeatedly referred to the Prophet of Islam صلى الله عليه وآله by the name "Ahmad" is his uncle, "Abu Talib." Today, in the book known as "Diwan Abi Talib" available to us, many poems are seen in which the noble Prophet of Islam is mentioned by the title "Ahmad"(28), such as:

ارادوا قتل احمد ظالموهم و لیس بقتلهم فیهم زعیم The oppressors decided to kill Ahmad, but they found no leader among them for his killing.

C) This expression is also seen in the poems of "Hassan ibn Thabit," the famous poet of the Prophet's era:

مفجعة قد شفها فقد احمد فظلت لآلاء الرّسول تعدّد A bereaved woman, whose loss of Ahmad had made her restless, continuously enumerated the gifts and blessings of the Messenger.(29)

We read in a Hadith that the Prophet of Islam صلى الله عليه وآله said: "My name on Earth is 'Muhammad,' and in the Heavens, it is 'Ahmad'."(30)

23) Gospel of John, Chapter 14, verse 16. 24) Gospel of John, Chapter 15, verse 26. 25) Gospel of John, Chapter 16, verse 8. 26) Tafsir Furqan. 27) Great French Encyclopedia, Vol. 23, p. 4176, quoted from Tafsir Namuneh. 28) Diwan Abi Talib, pp. 25 to 29, and Tarikh Ibn Asakir, Vol. 1, p. 275, quoted from Tafsir Namuneh. 29) Diwan Hassan ibn Thabit, p. 59, quoted from Tafsir Namuneh. 30) Tafsir Burhan.

Messages

  1. The history of the life of Jesus (عليه السلام) is noteworthy and memorable. «و اذ قال عیسى»

  2. Jesus is the Messenger of God; do not consider Him God. «انّى رسول اللّه»

  3. In divine education, there are multiple classes, where each class is a prerequisite for the next, and each teacher confirms the previous teacher. «و مصدّقاً لما بین یدىّ من التوراة و مبشّراً برسول...»

  4. Islam is the most complete of religions, because a prophecy (Bisharah) is always concerning something better and more complete. «و مبشّراً برسول»

  5. Among the friends of God, instead of envy, there is good tidings (Bisharah). «مبشّراً برسول من بعدى»

  6. Jesus is both the subject of good tidings (Mubashshar) and the bearer of good tidings (Mubashshir). He gives tidings of the coming of the Prophet of Islam «مبشّراً برسول...» and God gives tidings of His existence. «یا مریم ان اللّه یبشّرك بكلمة منه اسمه المسیح»(31)

  7. In invitations (to faith), pay attention to the past «مصدقاً...», consider your present time, «انّى رسول اللّه الیكم» and also keep the future in mind. «... من بعدى اسمه احمد»

  8. When introducing individuals, act in a way that leaves no ambiguity. «... اسمه احمد»

  9. Obstinacy is an element that causes neither previous prophecies nor existing signs to be effective. «فلمّا جاءهم بالبیّنات قالوا هذا سحرٌ مبین»

31) Aal-i-Imran, 45.

Ayah

وَمَنْ أَظْلَمُ مِمَّنِ ٱفْتَرَىٰ عَلَى ٱللَّهِ ٱلْكَذِبَ وَهُوَ يُدْعَىٰٓ إِلَى ٱلْإِسْلَـٰمِ ۚ وَٱللَّهُ لَا يَهْدِى ٱلْقَوْمَ ٱلظَّـٰلِمِينَ (۷)
Who is a greater wrongdoer than him who fabricates falsehoods against Allah, while he is being summoned to Islam? And Allah does not guide the wrongdoing lot. (7)

Key Points

| In the Quran, the word "اظلم" (most unjust/greatest wrongdoer) has been used sixteen times, fifteen of which relate to ideological and cultural issues, such as: "افترى على اللّه" (who invents a lie against Allah) 9 times, "كتم شهادة" (who conceals testimony) 1 time, "كذّب بآیات اللّه" (who denies the signs of Allah) 2 times, "منع مساجد اللّه" (who prevents [people] from the mosques of Allah) 1 time, "ذكّر بآیات ربّه فاعرض عنها" (who is reminded of the signs of his Lord, but turns away from them) 2 times. Oppression (zulm) is of three types: oppression against oneself, oppression against people, and oppression against the school (of thought/ideology). Among these, oppression against the religion and the school (of thought) is the most important and greatest type of oppression.(32)

32) Tafsir Atayeb al-Bayan.

Messages

1- The person who accepts neither the glad tidings of Jesus nor the miracles of the Prophet of Islam, and considers all of them outright magic, has committed the greatest slander and is the most unjust individual. «سحر مبین و من اظلم ممن افترى»

2- Slander against the Divine Prophets is slander against Allah. They said to the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله: «سحر مبین» (clear magic), [and] Allah identifies their action as slander against Him.

3- Slander against Allah and the religion of Allah is the worst type of slander. «و من اظلم ممن افترى على اللّه»

4- The greatest oppression is preventing the growth, knowledge, truth-seeking, and worship of the people. «من اظلم ممن افترى على اللّه»

5- The worst type of oppression is when the oppressor has been presented with the conclusive argument (proof), yet he still commits oppression. «و من اظلم... و هو یدعى الى الاسلام»

6- The result of slandering Allah is deprivation of Divine guidance. «واللّه لایهدى القوم الظالمین»

7- Guidance (hidayah) and misguidance (dalalah) are in the hand of Allah, but man prepares or destroys the grounds for them through his own choice and will. «لایهدى القوم الظالمین»

Ayah

يُرِيدُونَ لِيُطْفِـُٔوا۟ نُورَ ٱللَّهِ بِأَفْوَٰهِهِمْ وَٱللَّهُ مُتِمُّ نُورِهِۦ وَلَوْ كَرِهَ ٱلْكَـٰفِرُونَ (۸)
They desire to put out the light of Allah with their mouths, but Allah will perfect His light though the faithless should be averse. (8)

Ayah

هُوَ ٱلَّذِىٓ أَرْسَلَ رَسُولَهُۥ بِٱلْهُدَىٰ وَدِينِ ٱلْحَقِّ لِيُظْهِرَهُۥ عَلَى ٱلدِّينِ كُلِّهِۦ وَلَوْ كَرِهَ ٱلْمُشْرِكُونَ (۹)
It is He who has sent His Apostle with guidance and the true religion that He may make it prevail over all religions though the polytheists should be averse. (9)

Key Points

Similar to Ayahs 8 and 9 of this Surah, [this theme] is mentioned in Ayahs 32 and 33 of Surah At-Tawbah. The enemy uses various methods to extinguish the Light of Allah, including: - They accuse [it] of being reactionary and outdated. «اساطیر الاولین» (33) - They say these words are mere fantasy and delusion. «اضغاث احلام» (34) - They call the Prophet's words lies and fabrication. «ام یقولون افتراه» (35) - They deem the verses of revelation superficial and worthless. «لو نشاء لقلنا مثل هذا» (36) - They invent rivals for Allah. «و جعلوا للّه انداداً» (37) - They prevent people from hearing the divine verses. «لاتسمعوا لهذا القرآن و الغوا فیه» (38) - They mock the path and school of thought of the believers and humiliate them. «و طعنوا فى دینكم» (39) - They ridicule the religion of God. «اتخذوا دینكم هزواً و لعباً» (40) - They strive to destroy mosques and the centers of the school of thought. «سعى فى خرابها» (41) - They attempt to distort the religion. «یُحرّفون الكلم» (42) - Sometimes they introduce innovations (bid'ah) into the religion. «و رهبانیة ابتدعوها» (43) - Sometimes they conceal the truths of the religion. «یكتمون ما أنزلنا من البیّنات» (44) - They fragment the religion of Allah. «افتؤمنون ببعض الكتاب و تكفرون ببعض» (45) - They mix truth with falsehood. «ولاتلبسوا الحق بالباطل» (46) - They exaggerate (commit ghuluww) in the religion of God. «لاتغلوا فى دینكم» (47) - They rise up in war and conflict against Muslims. «لایزالون یقاتلونكم حتّى یردّوكم عن دینكم» (48)

Allah has promised the victory of Islam over all religions three times: «هو الّذى ارسل رسوله بالهدى و دین الحقّ لیظهره على الدین كلّه ولوكره المشركون» (49) «هو الّذى ارسل رسوله بالهدى و دین الحقّ لیظهره على الدین كلّه و كفى باللّه شهیداً» (50) «هو الّذى ارسل رسوله بالهدى و دین الحقّ لیظهره على الدین كلّه ولو كره المشركون» (51) And undoubtedly, Allah fulfills His promise. «و لن یخلف اللّه وعده» (52)

In undoubtedly mutawatir (frequently narrated) traditions, we read that the victory of Islam over other heavenly religions will be realized during the advent of Hadrat Mahdi عليه السلام. Hadrat Ali عليه السلام said: «فو الّذى نفسى بیده حتى لاتبقى قریة الاّ و ینادى فیها بشهادة ان لا اله الاّ اللّه بكرةً و عشیاً» (53) By the One in Whose Hand is my soul, no city or village will remain except that the sound of the Adhan and the two testimonies (Shahadatayn) will be heard therein every morning and evening.

The history of Islam has proven the realization of Ayah 8, because although the enemy has not hesitated for a moment—through ridicule, harassment and torture, economic and social siege, imposing wars, internal conspiracies by hypocrites, creating division among Muslims, launching the Crusades, promoting immorality and vices, and military and political colonialism—Islam has expanded day by day.

33) An'am, 25. 34) Anbiya, 5. 35) Ahqaf, 8. 36) Anfal, 31. 37) Ibrahim, 30. 38) Fussilat, 26. 39) Tawbah, 12. 40) Ma'idah, 57. 41) Baqarah, 114. 42) Nisa, 46. 43) Hadid, 27. 44) Baqarah, 159. 45) Baqarah, 85. 46) Baqarah, 42. 47) Ma'idah, 77. 48) Baqarah, 217. 49) Tawbah, 33. 50) Fath, 28. 51) Saff, 9. 52) Rum, 6; Hajj, 47. 53) Tafsirs Majma' al-Bayan, Kanz ad-Daqa'iq, and Burhan.

Messages

1- The enemy is constantly thinking of extinguishing the lamp of guidance and the Light of Allah. «یریدون لیطفئوا نور اللّه»

2- The Quran is the Divine Light. «لیطفئوا نور اللّه»

3- Religion is light; meaning it is the source of knowledge, growth, movement, and warmth. «نور اللّه»

4- The plans and decisions of the enemies to confront the truth are desperate and fruitless efforts. «نور اللّه بافواههم»

When can the sun be obscured by a puff [of breath]? When can the sea be defiled by the muzzle of a dog? The moon sheds light while the dog barks; Everyone acts according to his own nature.

5- The enemy's most important tool for confronting Islam is propaganda. «بافواههم» Of course, it will not succeed.

The lamp that God lights, Whoever puffs at it will burn his beard.

6- Islam is the complete and eternal religion. «واللّه متمّ نوره»

7- No type of opposition can prevent the Divine Will, neither polytheism «و لو كره المشركون» (54), nor disbelief. «ولو كره الكافرون»

8- Disbelief (Kufr) and polytheism (Shirk) are equally displeased with the expansion of Islam. «ولو كره الكافرون», «و لو كره المشركون»

9- Messengers are chosen by Divine Will, not by the choice and will of the people. «هو الّذى ارسل رسوله»

10- The victory of Islam is due to its truthfulness. «بالهدى و دین الحق لیظهره»

11- The future belongs to Islam. «لیظهره على الدین كلّه»

12- The school of Islam is hope-inspiring. «لیظهره على الدین كلّه»

13- A sovereignty and dominance is valuable only if it is complete and permanent. «لیظهره على الدین كلّه»

14- Except for Islam, all religions have an expiration date, and Islam alone is the eternal school of thought. «لیظهره على الدین كلّه»

15- The factors for the victory of Islam are several:

A) Divine Will and assistance, «هو الّذى» B) Infallible and worthy leadership, «رسوله» C) The natural need of society, «بالهدى » D) Possessing truthfulness, «دین الحق» E) The comprehensive and complete nature of the school of thought, «متمّ نوره» (54)

Ayah

يَـٰٓأَيُّهَا ٱلَّذِينَ ءَامَنُوا۟ هَلْ أَدُلُّكُمْ عَلَىٰ تِجَـٰرَةٍ تُنجِيكُم مِّنْ عَذَابٍ أَلِيمٍ (۱٠)
O you who have faith! Shall I show you a deal that will deliver you from a painful punishment? (10)

Ayah

تُؤْمِنُونَ بِٱللَّهِ وَرَسُولِهِۦ وَتُجَـٰهِدُونَ فِى سَبِيلِ ٱللَّهِ بِأَمْوَٰلِكُمْ وَأَنفُسِكُمْ ۚ ذَٰلِكُمْ خَيْرٌ لَّكُمْ إِن كُنتُمْ تَعْلَمُونَ (۱۱)
Have faith in Allah and His Apostle, and wage jihad in the way of Allah with your persons and possessions. That is better for you, should you know. (11)

Ayah

يَغْفِرْ لَكُمْ ذُنُوبَكُمْ وَيُدْخِلْكُمْ جَنَّـٰتٍ تَجْرِى مِن تَحْتِهَا ٱلْأَنْهَـٰرُ وَمَسَـٰكِنَ طَيِّبَةً فِى جَنَّـٰتِ عَدْنٍ ۚ ذَٰلِكَ ٱلْفَوْزُ ٱلْعَظِيمُ (۱۲)
He will forgive your sins and admit you into gardens with streams running in them, and into good dwellings in the Gardens of Eden; that is the great success. (12)

Key Points

In the previous Ayah, the dominance of the religion of Islam over other religions was promised, but the fulfillment of this promise requires three conditions: a) A complete and universal law, which is the Quran, a light that Allah has perfected and which no one has the power to extinguish. b) The existence of an infallible leader, who is Hadrat Mahdi, Baqiyyatullah (the remainder of Allah), and the Divine reserve. c) The readiness of the people, which is discussed in this Ayah. These Ayat are equivalent in weight to Ayah 111 of Surah At-Tawbah, which states: «انّ اللّه اشترى من المؤمنین أنفسهم و أموالهم بانّ لهم الجنّة...»

There are several possibilities in trade: 1. Bad trade: «بئسما اشتروا به أنفسهم»(55) 2. Trade without profit: «فما ربحت تجارتهم»(56) 3. Low-profit trade: «متاع الدنیا قلیل»(57) 4. Loss-making trade: «انّ الخاسرین الّذین خسروا أنفسهم»(58) 5. Extremely loss-making trade: «انّ الانسان لفى خُسر»(59) 6. Disgraceful trade: «الخسران المبین»(60) 7. Stagnant trade: «تخشون كسادها»(61) 8. Continuous and lasting trade: «تجارة لن تبور»(62) 9. Saving trade: «تجارة تنجیكم»(63) 10. Profitable trade: «تجارة مُربِحة»(64)

Hadrat Ali عليه السلام says: Your price is Paradise, so do not sell yourselves cheaply: «لیس لانفسكم ثمنٌ الاّ الجنّة فلا تبیعوها الاّ بها»(65) The world is the marketplace of Allah's saints: «الدنیا متجر اولیاء اللّه»(66) It is interesting that Hadrat Ali عليه السلام said this to someone who was speaking ill of the world. In trade with Allah, we take the capital from Him and sell it back to Him at a high price.

In Islamic culture, many material matters have spiritual counterparts. For example: A) Material food: «كلوا من الطیبات»(67) Spiritual food: «فلینظر الانسان الى طعامه»(68), which the Imam عليه السلام interprets as: «الى علمه»(69), meaning, let him see what he learns. B) Material clothing: «سرابیل تقیكم»(70) Spiritual clothing: «و لباس التقوى ذلك خیر»(71) C) Material adornment: «خذوا زینتكم عند كل مسجد»(72) Spiritual adornment: «حبّب الیكم الایمان و زیّنه فى قلوبكم»(73) D) Material journey: «و ان كنتم على سفر»(74) Spiritual journey: «انك كادح الى ربّك كدحاً»(75) E) Material provision (Rizq): «من الثّمرات رزقاً لكم»(76) Spiritual provision: «وارزقنى حج بیتك الحرام»(77) F) Material sweetness: «حلاوة الدنیا»(78) Spiritual sweetness: «حلاوة الایمان»(79), «حلاوة وُدّك»(80) G) Material reward: «جنات تجرى من تحتها الانهار...»(81) Spiritual reward: «و رضوان من اللّه اكبر»(82) H) Material father: «و والد و ما ولد»(83) Spiritual father: «أنا و علىٌ ابوا هذه الامّة»(84) I) Material migration (Hijrah): «و هاجروا»(85) Spiritual migration: «و الرّجز فاهجر»(86)

We read in a Hadith that Ali عليه السلام said: «أنا التّجارة المربحة المنجیة من العذاب الالیم التّى دلّ اللّه علیها فى كتابه فقال «هل أدلّكم على تجارةٍ...»»(87) I am that profitable trade which leads to salvation from the painful punishment, the very one that Allah referred to in the Quran.

(Mentioned 27 times in the Quran). 82) Tawbah, 72. 83) Balad, 3. 84) Bihar, Vol. 36, p. 11. 85) Anfal, 72. 86) Muddaththir, 5. 87) Tafsir Kanz ad-Daqa’iq.

Messages

  1. We should address people using their title and designation of faith. «یا أیّها الّذین آمنوا»

  2. We must first create readiness in people, and one way to do this is by posing a question. «هل أدلّكم»

  3. Trade requires a guide. «هل أدلّكم على تجارة»

  4. Spiritual trade is not understandable to everyone; only those who believe in Allah grasp this meaning. «یا أیّها الّذین آمنوا هل أدلّكم...»

  5. Man is instinctively seeking profit, especially stable and lasting profit. «هل أدلّكم على تجارة تنجیكم»

  6. True profit is salvation from Divine wrath. «تجارة تنجیكم من عذاب الیم»

  7. Averting danger is more important than attracting benefit. «تجارة تنجیكم من عذاب الیم»

  8. Trade is not limited to worldly affairs. «تجارة... تؤمنون باللّه»

  9. Following the Prophets is a highly profitable trade. «تجارةٍ... تؤمنون باللّه و رسوله»

  10. Faith has different levels. «یا أیّها الّذین آمنوا... تؤمنون»

  11. In propagating religion, one must use titles and subjects that are of interest to people. The subject of trade and earning income is relevant to everyone; hence, faith in Allah is presented as a highly profitable trade. «تجارةٍ... تؤمنون باللّه و رسوله»

  12. Faith is not merely a belief in the heart; it requires effort and Jihad. «تؤمنون... تجاهدون»

  13. Faith in Allah is accompanied by faith in the Messenger, and Jihad with wealth is accompanied by Jihad with life. «تؤمنون... تجاهدون...»

  14. Seasonal, temporary, or intermittent faith and Jihad are not effective; continuity is required. «تؤمنون... تجاهدون...» (The present tense verb indicates permanence and continuity.)

  15. Superficial faith does not lead to important deeds; complete faith is necessary. «یا أیّها الّذین آمنوا... تؤمنون... تجاهدون»

  16. Faith precedes action. «تؤمنون... تجاهدون»

  17. Preserving religion is superior to preserving wealth and life, and one must sacrifice wealth and life to preserve religion. «تجاهدون فى سبیل اللّه باموالكم و أنفسكم»

  18. Jihad is not lifted from any individual; sometimes one must strive with wealth, sometimes with life, and sometimes with both wealth and life. «تجاهدون... باموالكم و أنفسكم»

  19. In instructions, we should start with lighter tasks and move toward heavier ones. (Jihad with wealth is mentioned before Jihad with life). «باموالكم و أنفسكم»

  20. The value of deeds lies in sincerity. «فى سبیل اللّه»

  21. Many hardships may be beneficial to us, though we do not know it. «تجاهدون... خیر لكم»

  22. Man instinctively seeks goodness and happiness, and Allah has promised the attainment of it. «ذلكم خیر لكم»

  23. Paradise is given for a price, not an excuse. «تؤمنون... تجاهدون... یدخلكم جنّات»

  24. Complete action leads to complete reward. (In these Ayat, the issue of faith in Allah and the Messenger and Jihad with wealth and life is presented on one hand, and the forgiveness of all sins, entry into Paradise, and the great triumph on the other.) «یغفر لكم... جنّات... مساكن... ذلك الفوز العظیم»

  25. Receiving Divine favors requires purity and purification. (First forgiveness, then Paradise and great salvation). «یغفر لكم... یدخلكم... ذلك الفوز العظیم»

  26. Man is situated between painful punishment and great triumph, and through a transaction with Allah, he is released from the punishment and attains the triumph. «تنجیكم من عذاب الیم... ذلك الفوز العظیم»

Ayah

وَأُخْرَىٰ تُحِبُّونَهَا ۖ نَصْرٌ مِّنَ ٱللَّهِ وَفَتْحٌ قَرِيبٌ ۗ وَبَشِّرِ ٱلْمُؤْمِنِينَ (۱۳)
And other [blessings] you cherish: help from Allah and a victory near at hand, and give good news to the faithful. (13)

Key Points

Entry into Paradise and divine forgiveness are instances of salvation from divine punishment, but since divine help (Nusrah) and victory (Fath) are another favor, Allah has mentioned them separately.

Messages

1- Not all rewards are in the Hereafter; some of them are in this world. «واُخرى تحبّونها...»

2- The reward must be desirable to the one being encouraged. «تحبّونها»

3- Sincere combatants achieve one of two good things: if they are martyred: «یدخلكم جنّات...» (which was in the previous Ayah), and if they are victorious: «نصر من اللّه و فتح قریب»

4- Attribute victory to Allah, not to equipment, experience, or expertise. «نصرٌ من اللّه»

5- Hope for a bright future is a means of encouragement for the combatants. «نصرٌ، فتح»

6- Allah fulfills His promises:

Here: «نصر من اللّه» and elsewhere: «اذا جاء نصر اللّه و الفتح »(88)

Here: «فتح قریب» and elsewhere: «انّا فتحنا لك فتحاً مبیناً»(89)

7- To encourage combatants to sacrifice their wealth and lives, use the leverage of glad tidings. «و بشّر المؤمنین» (88) An-Nasr, 1.

(89) Al-Fath, 1.

Ayah

يَـٰٓأَيُّهَا ٱلَّذِينَ ءَامَنُوا۟ كُونُوٓا۟ أَنصَارَ ٱللَّهِ كَمَا قَالَ عِيسَى ٱبْنُ مَرْيَمَ لِلْحَوَارِيِّـۧنَ مَنْ أَنصَارِىٓ إِلَى ٱللَّهِ ۖ قَالَ ٱلْحَوَارِيُّونَ نَحْنُ أَنصَارُ ٱللَّهِ ۖ فَـَٔامَنَت طَّآئِفَةٌ مِّنۢ بَنِىٓ إِسْرَٰٓءِيلَ وَكَفَرَت طَّآئِفَةٌ ۖ فَأَيَّدْنَا ٱلَّذِينَ ءَامَنُوا۟ عَلَىٰ عَدُوِّهِمْ فَأَصْبَحُوا۟ ظَـٰهِرِينَ (۱٤)
O you who have faith! Be Allah’s helpers, just as Jesus son of Mary said to his disciples, ‘Who will be my helpers for Allah’s sake?’ The Disciples said, ‘We will be Allah’s helpers!’ So a group of the Children of Israel believed, and a group disbelieved. Then We strengthened the faithful against their enemies, and they came to prevail [over them]. (14)

Key Points

«Al-Hawariyyūn» (the Disciples) refers to the special companions of Prophet Jesus (عليه السلام), who were twelve in number, and their names are mentioned in the current Gospels. (90) The root of this word is from ‘Hūr’, meaning to wash and whiten. This term was applied to them either because they wore white clothes and possessed pure hearts, or because they themselves had pure inner selves and also invited others to purity. Imam Sadiq (عليه السلام) said: Our Shī‘a are our Disciples. The Disciples of Jesus (عليه السلام) did not help him against his enemies and the Jews, but the Shī‘a help us, and they are martyred, tortured, or exiled in our path. May Allah grant them a good reward in return for all the hardships they endure for our sake. «ینصروننا و یقاتلون دوننا و یحرّقون و یعذّبون و یشرّدون فى البلاد جزاهم اللّه عنّا خیراً» (91) Citing similar instances is effective in accepting an invitation and performing an action. Just as Jesus had helpers, «كما قال عیسى...» (as Jesus said...), and just as fasting was made obligatory upon others. «كما كتب على الّذین من قبلكم» (92)

90) Tafsir Namūneh. 91) Tafsir Kanz al-Daqā’iq. 92) Baqarah, 183.

Messages

1- The believer must grow step by step. In the previous verses, there was an invitation to trade with Allah; in this verse, He formally invites them to be the Helpers of Allah, «كونوا أنصار اللّه» (meaning: be steadfast forever in supporting the Divine law and the Divine authorities).

2- Allah has no need for our help, because all help and victories come from Him. «نصر من اللّه» (Help is from Allah). But being the Helpers of Allah is an honor for us.

«كونوا أنصار اللّه»

3- The Divine Prophets desire people for the sake of Allah, not for themselves or their own group or party. «مَن أنصارى الى اللّه» (Who are my helpers toward Allah?)

4- To overcome the enemy, the Prophets followed ordinary and natural paths and utilized apparent means and popular forces. «مَن أنصارى الى اللّه»

5- A leader must have an accurate assessment of his companions. «مَن أنصارى الى اللّه»

6- Obtaining affirmation from companions is a type of renewal of allegiance and a declaration of loyalty.

«مَن أنصارى... قال الحواریون نحن أنصار اللّه»

7- We should respond to the call of religious leaders. «مَن أنصارى... نحن أنصار اللّه»

8- Man is free to choose whether to accept the invitation of the Prophets and become prosperous, or to reject it and become wretched. «فآمنت طائفةٌ... و كفرت طائفة» (So a party believed... and a party disbelieved.)

9- Usually, not all people believed in a single Prophet; rather, some disbelieved and some believed. We should not expect everyone to believe either. «فآمنت طائفةٌ... و كفرت طائفة»

10- Possessing faith is the key to receiving Divine confirmations. «فآمنت... فایّدنا...» (So they believed... then We supported...)

11- Prophet Jesus (عليه السلام) himself was confirmed by Allah, «و أیّدناه بروح القدس» (and We supported him with the Holy Spirit) (93), and so were his special companions. «فایّدنا الّذین آمنوا على عدوّهم فاصبحوا ظاهرین» (Then We supported those who believed against their enemy, and they became dominant.)

12- Just as Allah confirms and helps His Prophet, «و أیّدناه بروح القدس», He also helps His followers. «فایّدنا الّذین آمنوا»

93) Baqarah, 78.
